text: A well-designed API is crucial for smooth software integration. When building REST APIs, your endpoints should be clear and consistent, using nouns for resources like `/users` or `/products`. Always use appropriate HTTP status codes; for example, 200 for success, 201 for creation, and 404 for not-found errors. Good documentation is essential, explaining how to use each endpoint with examples. Finally, plan for changes by including versioning in your URL, such as `/v1/users`. This way, you can update your API without breaking existing applications that rely on older versions. Following these best practices creates a reliable and developer-friendly API.
